Loading packages
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(rstatix)
```

Reading relevant csvs
```{r}
# Read the data files
flowers <- read.csv("~/Documents/repos/turbpe/spreadsheets/flowers_number_coded_nativity.csv")
bees <- read.csv("~/Documents/repos/turbpe/spreadsheets/bee_captures.csv")
```

```{r}
# Clean the data
# Remove rows with missing nativity values in flowers data
flowers_clean <- flowers %>%
  filter(!is.na(nativity)) %>%
  mutate(Type = "Flowers")

# Clean bee data - remove rows with missing nativity
bees_clean <- bees %>%
  filter(!is.na(nativity)) %>%
  mutate(Type = "Bees")

# Calculate proportions for flowers
flowers_summary <- flowers_clean %>%
  group_by(nativity) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    proportion = n() / nrow(flowers_clean) * 100
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    nativity_status = case_when(
      nativity == 1 ~ "Native",
      nativity == 0 ~ "Non-native"
    ),
    Type = "Flowers"
  )

# Calculate proportions for bees
bees_summary <- bees_clean %>%
  group_by(nativity) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    proportion = n() / nrow(bees_clean) * 100
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    nativity_status = case_when(
      nativity == 1 ~ "Native",
      nativity == 0 ~ "Non-native"
    ),
    Type = "Bees"
  )

# Print summary statistics
cat("========== FLOWER OBSERVATIONS ==========\n")
cat("Total observations:", nrow(flowers_clean), "\n")
print(flowers_summary)
cat("\n")

cat("========== BEE CAPTURES ==========\n")
cat("Total captures:", nrow(bees_clean), "\n")
print(bees_summary)
cat("\n")

# Prepare data for statistical testing
# Create a combined dataset for flowers
flowers_individual <- flowers_clean %>%
  select(nativity, Type) %>%
  mutate(nativity_status = ifelse(nativity == 1, "Native", "Non-native"))

# Create a combined dataset for bees
bees_individual <- bees_clean %>%
  select(nativity, Type) %>%
  mutate(nativity_status = ifelse(nativity == 1, "Native", "Non-native"))

# Combine datasets
combined_data <- bind_rows(flowers_individual, bees_individual)

# Create a contingency table for chi-square test
contingency_table <- table(combined_data$Type, combined_data$nativity_status)
cat("========== CONTINGENCY TABLE ==========\n")
print(contingency_table)
cat("\n")

# Chi-square test to compare proportions between flowers and bees
chi_square_result <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
cat("========== CHI-SQUARE TEST RESULTS ==========\n")
cat("Chi-square statistic:", chi_square_result$statistic, "\n")
cat("Degrees of freedom:", chi_square_result$parameter, "\n")
cat("P-value:", chi_square_result$p.value, "\n")

# Calculate effect size (Cramer's V)
cramer_v <- sqrt(chi_square_result$statistic / 
                   (sum(contingency_table) * (min(dim(contingency_table)) - 1)))
cat("Cramer's V (effect size):", cramer_v, "\n")

# Interpret p-value
if(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  cat("Interpretation: Highly significant difference (p < 0.001)\n")
} else if(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  cat("Interpretation: Very significant difference (p < 0.01)\n")
} else if(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("Interpretation: Significant difference (p < 0.05)\n")
} else {
  cat("Interpretation: No significant difference (p >= 0.05)\n")
}
cat("\n")

# Calculate proportions with confidence intervals for boxplot
proportion_data <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(Type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_native = sum(nativity_status == "Native"),
    n_non_native = sum(nativity_status == "Non-native"),
    total = n(),
    proportion_native = n_native / total * 100,
    proportion_non_native = n_non_native / total * 100
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(proportion_native, proportion_non_native),
               names_to = "nativity_status",
               values_to = "proportion") %>%
  mutate(
    nativity_status = ifelse(nativity_status == "proportion_native", "Native", "Non-native"),
    nativity_status = factor(nativity_status, levels = c("Native", "Non-native")),
    Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Flowers", "Bees"))
  )

# Bootstrap for confidence intervals
set.seed(123)
bootstrap_props <- function(data, type_name, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
  type_data <- data %>% filter(Type == type_name)
  n_total <- nrow(type_data)
  
  native_props <- replicate(n_bootstrap, {
    boot_sample <- sample(type_data$nativity_status, n_total, replace = TRUE)
    sum(boot_sample == "Native") / n_total * 100
  })
  
  non_native_props <- replicate(n_bootstrap, {
    boot_sample <- sample(type_data$nativity_status, n_total, replace = TRUE)
    sum(boot_sample == "Non-native") / n_total * 100
  })
  
  return(list(
    native = quantile(native_props, c(0.025, 0.975)),
    non_native = quantile(non_native_props, c(0.025, 0.975))
  ))
}

flowers_ci <- bootstrap_props(combined_data, "Flowers")
bees_ci <- bootstrap_props(combined_data, "Bees")

# Add confidence intervals to proportion_data
proportion_data <- proportion_data %>%
  mutate(
    lower_ci = case_when(
      Type == "Flowers" & nativity_status == "Native" ~ flowers_ci$native[1],
      Type == "Flowers" & nativity_status == "Non-native" ~ flowers_ci$non_native[1],
      Type == "Bees" & nativity_status == "Native" ~ bees_ci$native[1],
      Type == "Bees" & nativity_status == "Non-native" ~ bees_ci$non_native[1]
    ),
    upper_ci = case_when(
      Type == "Flowers" & nativity_status == "Native" ~ flowers_ci$native[2],
      Type == "Flowers" & nativity_status == "Non-native" ~ flowers_ci$non_native[2],
      Type == "Bees" & nativity_status == "Native" ~ bees_ci$native[2],
      Type == "Bees" & nativity_status == "Non-native" ~ bees_ci$non_native[2]
    )
  )

# Perform pairwise proportion tests
pairwise_test <- prop.test(x = c(sum(flowers_individual$nativity == 1), 
                                  sum(bees_individual$nativity == 1)),
                            n = c(nrow(flowers_individual), 
                                  nrow(bees_individual)))

cat("========== PAIRWISE PROPORTION TEST ==========\n")
cat("Flowers native proportion:", 
    sum(flowers_individual$nativity == 1)/nrow(flowers_individual) * 100, "%\n")
cat("Bees native proportion:", 
    sum(bees_individual$nativity == 1)/nrow(bees_individual) * 100, "%\n")
cat("Difference:", abs(sum(flowers_individual$nativity == 1)/nrow(flowers_individual) - 
                        sum(bees_individual$nativity == 1)/nrow(bees_individual)) * 100, "%\n")
cat("P-value:", pairwise_test$p.value, "\n")
cat("95% Confidence interval for difference:", 
    round(pairwise_test$conf.int[1] * 100, 2), "% to", 
    round(pairwise_test$conf.int[2] * 100, 2), "%\n")
cat("\n")

# Create the boxplot figure
p <- ggplot(proportion_data, aes(x = Type, y = proportion, fill = nativity_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
           width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.25, 
                size = 0.8) +
  # Add significance annotation
  annotate("text", x = 1.5, y = max(proportion_data$upper_ci) + 5,
           label = ifelse(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                         ifelse(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(chi_square_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "n.s."))),
           size = 8, fontface = "bold") +
  # Add bracket for significance
  annotate("segment", x = 1.2, xend = 1.8, 
           y = max(proportion_data$upper_ci) + 3,
           yend = max(proportion_data$upper_ci) + 3,
           size = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Native vs exotic proportions of flower/bee observations",
    subtitle = paste0("Flowers vs Bees | Chi-square p-value: ", 
                      format(chi_square_result$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)),
    x = "Observation Type",
    y = "Proportion (%)",
    fill = "Nativity Status"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native" = "#265380", "Non-native" = "#E3DE2D")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(proportion_data$upper_ci) + 10),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

# Display the plot
print(p)

# Save the plot
#ggsave("nativity_comparison_boxplot.png", p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Additional analysis: Test within each group if proportions differ from 50:50
cat("========== WITHIN-GROUP ANALYSES ==========\n")

# Test if flower observations deviate from 50:50 native:non-native
flower_binom <- binom.test(sum(flowers_individual$nativity == 1), 
                           nrow(flowers_individual), p = 0.5)
cat("Flowers - Binomial test (50:50 expectation):\n")
cat("  P-value:", flower_binom$p.value, "\n")
cat("  95% CI for native proportion:", 
    round(flower_binom$conf.int[1] * 100, 2), "% to",
    round(flower_binom$conf.int[2] * 100, 2), "%\n\n")

# Test if bee captures deviate from 50:50 native:non-native
bee_binom <- binom.test(sum(bees_individual$nativity == 1), 
                        nrow(bees_individual), p = 0.5)
cat("Bees - Binomial test (50:50 expectation):\n")
cat("  P-value:", bee_binom$p.value, "\n")
cat("  95% CI for native proportion:", 
    round(bee_binom$conf.int[1] * 100, 2), "% to",
    round(bee_binom$conf.int[2] * 100, 2), "%\n\n")

# Additional visualization: Stacked bar chart
p_stacked <- ggplot(proportion_data, aes(x = Type, y = proportion, fill = nativity_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(proportion, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Native vs Non-native Composition",
    x = "Observation Type",
    y = "Proportion (%)",
    fill = "Nativity Status"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native" = "#265380", "Non-native" = "#E3DE2D")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(p_stacked)
#ggsave("nativity_stacked_barchart.png", p_stacked, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

Comparing what proportion of native bees landed on native vs non-native flowers
to that of non-native bees
```{r}
# Bee-flower interactions analysis by nativity

# Merge bee captures with flower nativity data
# First, rename columns to avoid confusion
flowers_for_merge <- flowers_clean %>%
  select(Species_ID, nativity) %>%
  rename(flower_nativity = nativity,
         Plant_ID = Species_ID) %>%
  distinct(Plant_ID, flower_nativity)  # Keep unique plant IDs

# Join bee data with flower nativity
bee_flower_interactions <- bees_clean %>%
  left_join(flowers_for_merge, by = "Plant_ID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(flower_nativity)) %>%  # Remove if plant ID not found in flower data
  mutate(
    bee_nativity_status = ifelse(nativity == 1, "Native Bees", "Non-native Bees"),
    flower_nativity_status = ifelse(flower_nativity == 1, "Native Flowers", "Non-native Flowers")
  )

# Summary statistics for interactions
cat("========== BEE-FLOWER INTERACTION SUMMARY ==========\n")
cat("Total bee-flower interactions (with known flower nativity):", nrow(bee_flower_interactions), "\n\n")

# Breakdown by bee nativity and flower nativity
interaction_summary <- bee_flower_interactions %>%
  group_by(bee_nativity_status, flower_nativity_status) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  group_by(bee_nativity_status) %>%
  mutate(
    proportion = count / sum(count) * 100,
    total_interactions = sum(count)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

print(interaction_summary)
cat("\n")

# Calculate proportions for each bee group
native_bee_interactions <- bee_flower_interactions %>%
  filter(bee_nativity_status == "Native Bees") %>%
  summarise(
    native_flowers = sum(flower_nativity == 1),
    non_native_flowers = sum(flower_nativity == 0),
    total = n(),
    prop_native_flowers = native_flowers / total * 100,
    prop_non_native_flowers = non_native_flowers / total * 100
  )

nonnative_bee_interactions <- bee_flower_interactions %>%
  filter(bee_nativity_status == "Non-native Bees") %>%
  summarise(
    native_flowers = sum(flower_nativity == 1),
    non_native_flowers = sum(flower_nativity == 0),
    total = n(),
    prop_native_flowers = native_flowers / total * 100,
    prop_non_native_flowers = non_native_flowers / total * 100
  )

cat("========== NATIVE BEES ==========\n")
cat("Total interactions:", native_bee_interactions$total, "\n")
cat("Interactions with native flowers:", native_bee_interactions$native_flowers, 
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", native_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers), "\n")
cat("Interactions with non-native flowers:", native_bee_interactions$non_native_flowers, 
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", native_bee_interactions$prop_non_native_flowers), "\n\n")

cat("========== NON-NATIVE BEES ==========\n")
cat("Total interactions:", nonnative_bee_interactions$total, "\n")
cat("Interactions with native flowers:", nonnative_bee_interactions$native_flowers, 
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", nonnative_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers), "\n")
cat("Interactions with non-native flowers:", nonnative_bee_interactions$non_native_flowers, 
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", nonnative_bee_interactions$prop_non_native_flowers), "\n\n")

# Stats test for bee flower interactions

# Create contingency table for bee nativity vs flower nativity
interaction_contingency <- table(
  bee_flower_interactions$bee_nativity_status,
  bee_flower_interactions$flower_nativity_status
)

cat("========== CONTINGENCY TABLE ==========\n")
print(interaction_contingency)
cat("\n")

# Chi-square test for independence
interaction_chi <- chisq.test(interaction_contingency)
cat("========== CHI-SQUARE TEST RESULTS ==========\n")
cat("Chi-square statistic:", interaction_chi$statistic, "\n")
cat("Degrees of freedom:", interaction_chi$parameter, "\n")
cat("P-value:", interaction_chi$p.value, "\n")

# Calculate effect size (Cramer's V)
interaction_cramers_v <- sqrt(interaction_chi$statistic / 
                                (sum(interaction_contingency) * 
                                   (min(dim(interaction_contingency)) - 1)))
cat("Cramer's V (effect size):", interaction_cramers_v, "\n")

# Interpret p-value
if(interaction_chi$p.value < 0.001) {
  cat("Interpretation: Highly significant difference in floral preferences between native and non-native bees (p < 0.001)\n")
} else if(interaction_chi$p.value < 0.01) {
  cat("Interpretation: Very significant difference in floral preferences between native and non-native bees (p < 0.01)\n")
} else if(interaction_chi$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("Interpretation: Significant difference in floral preferences between native and non-native bees (p < 0.05)\n")
} else {
  cat("Interpretation: No significant difference in floral preferences between native and non-native bees (p >= 0.05)\n")
}
cat("\n")

# Two-proportion test: Compare proportion of native flowers visited by native vs non-native bees
prop_test_result <- prop.test(
  x = c(native_bee_interactions$native_flowers, 
        nonnative_bee_interactions$native_flowers),
  n = c(native_bee_interactions$total, 
        nonnative_bee_interactions$total)
)

cat("========== TWO-PROPORTION TEST ==========\n")
cat("Comparing proportion of visits to NATIVE flowers:\n")
cat("Native bees:", sprintf("%.1f%%", native_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers), "\n")
cat("Non-native bees:", sprintf("%.1f%%", nonnative_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers), "\n")
cat("Difference:", sprintf("%.1f%%", abs(native_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers - 
                                           nonnative_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers)), "\n")
cat("P-value:", prop_test_result$p.value, "\n")
cat("95% CI for difference:", 
    round(prop_test_result$conf.int[1] * 100, 1), "% to", 
    round(prop_test_result$conf.int[2] * 100, 1), "%\n\n")

# Binomial test for each bee group (do they prefer native or non-native flowers?)
native_bee_binom <- binom.test(
  x = native_bee_interactions$native_flowers,
  n = native_bee_interactions$total,
  p = 0.5
)

nonnative_bee_binom <- binom.test(
  x = nonnative_bee_interactions$native_flowers,
  n = nonnative_bee_interactions$total,
  p = 0.5
)

cat("========== BINOMIAL TESTS (50:50 EXPECTATION) ==========\n")
cat("Native bees - do they prefer native flowers?\n")
cat("  P-value:", native_bee_binom$p.value, "\n")
cat("  95% CI for proportion visiting native flowers:", 
    round(native_bee_binom$conf.int[1] * 100, 1), "% to",
    round(native_bee_binom$conf.int[2] * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  Interpretation:", 
    ifelse(native_bee_binom$p.value < 0.05, 
           ifelse(native_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers > 50,
                  "Native bees significantly prefer native flowers",
                  "Native bees significantly prefer non-native flowers"),
           "Native bees show no significant preference"), "\n\n")

cat("Non-native bees - do they prefer native flowers?\n")
cat("  P-value:", nonnative_bee_binom$p.value, "\n")
cat("  95% CI for proportion visiting native flowers:", 
    round(nonnative_bee_binom$conf.int[1] * 100, 1), "% to",
    round(nonnative_bee_binom$conf.int[2] * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  Interpretation:", 
    ifelse(nonnative_bee_binom$p.value < 0.05,
           ifelse(nonnative_bee_interactions$prop_native_flowers > 50,
                  "Non-native bees significantly prefer native flowers",
                  "Non-native bees significantly prefer non-native flowers"),
           "Non-native bees show no significant preference"), "\n\n")

# Bootstrap confidence interval for proportions

set.seed(123)
bootstrap_interaction_props <- function(data, bee_group, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
  group_data <- data %>% filter(bee_nativity_status == bee_group)
  n_total <- nrow(group_data)
  
  native_flower_props <- replicate(n_bootstrap, {
    boot_sample <- sample(group_data$flower_nativity, n_total, replace = TRUE)
    sum(boot_sample == 1) / n_total * 100
  })
  
  return(quantile(native_flower_props, c(0.025, 0.975)))
}

native_bees_ci <- bootstrap_interaction_props(bee_flower_interactions, "Native Bees")
nonnative_bees_ci <- bootstrap_interaction_props(bee_flower_interactions, "Non-native Bees")


# Prepare data for plotting
interaction_plot_data <- interaction_summary %>%
  mutate(
    lower_ci = case_when(
      bee_nativity_status == "Native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Native Flowers" ~ native_bees_ci[1],
      bee_nativity_status == "Native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Non-native Flowers" ~ 100 - native_bees_ci[2],
      bee_nativity_status == "Non-native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Native Flowers" ~ nonnative_bees_ci[1],
      bee_nativity_status == "Non-native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Non-native Flowers" ~ 100 - nonnative_bees_ci[2]
    ),
    upper_ci = case_when(
      bee_nativity_status == "Native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Native Flowers" ~ native_bees_ci[2],
      bee_nativity_status == "Native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Non-native Flowers" ~ 100 - native_bees_ci[1],
      bee_nativity_status == "Non-native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Native Flowers" ~ nonnative_bees_ci[2],
      bee_nativity_status == "Non-native Bees" & flower_nativity_status == "Non-native Flowers" ~ 100 - nonnative_bees_ci[1]
    )
  )

# Plot 1: Bee-flower interaction preferences (WITHOUT the "n.s." annotation)
p_interactions <- ggplot(interaction_plot_data, 
                         aes(x = bee_nativity_status, y = proportion, fill = flower_nativity_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
           width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.25, 
                size = 0.8) +
  # REMOVED the annotate() function that was adding the "n.s." text
  # Also removed the segment annotation that created the bracket
  labs(
    title = "Floral Preferences of Native vs Non-native Bees",
    subtitle = paste0("Proportion of bee visits to native vs exotic flowers | Chi-square p-value: ", 
                      format(interaction_chi$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)),
    x = "Bee Nativity",
    y = "Proportion of Visits (%)",
    fill = "Flower Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native Flowers" = "#265380", "Non-native Flowers" = "#E3DE2D")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(interaction_plot_data$upper_ci) + 10),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

print(p_interactions)

# Plot 2: Stacked bar chart for bee-flower interactions
p_interactions_stacked <- ggplot(interaction_summary, 
                                 aes(x = bee_nativity_status, y = proportion, fill = flower_nativity_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(proportion, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Bee-Flower Interaction Composition",
    subtitle = paste0("Total interactions: Native bees = ", native_bee_interactions$total, 
                      ", Non-native bees = ", nonnative_bee_interactions$total),
    x = "Bee Nativity",
    y = "Proportion of Visits (%)",
    fill = "Flower Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native Flowers" = "#265380", "Non-native Flowers" = "#E3DE2D")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(p_interactions_stacked)

# Plot 3: Heatmap of interaction frequencies
p_heatmap <- ggplot(interaction_summary, 
                    aes(x = bee_nativity_status, y = flower_nativity_status, fill = count)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(count, "\n(", round(proportion, 1), "%)")), 
            size = 4.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#265380", high = "#E3DE2D", trans = "log10") +
  labs(
    title = "Bee-Flower Interaction Frequency Heatmap",
    x = "Bee Nativity",
    y = "Flower Type",
    fill = "Number of\nInteractions"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

print(p_heatmap)

# Flower preferences by bee family

cat("========== FLOWER PREFERENCES BY BEE FAMILY ==========\n")

family_interactions <- bee_flower_interactions %>%
  group_by(Family, bee_nativity_status, flower_nativity_status) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(Family, bee_nativity_status) %>%
  mutate(
    proportion = count / sum(count) * 100,
    total = sum(count)
  ) %>%
  filter(total >= 5)  # Only include families with at least 5 interactions

print(family_interactions)
cat("\n")

# Plot by bee family (if enough data)
if(nrow(family_interactions) > 0) {
  p_family <- ggplot(family_interactions %>% 
                      filter(flower_nativity_status == "Native Flowers"),
                    aes(x = reorder(Family, -proportion), y = proportion, fill = bee_nativity_status)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = "Proportion of Visits to Native Flowers by Bee Family",
      x = "Bee Family",
      y = "Visits to Native Flowers (%)",
      fill = "Bee Nativity"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native Bees" = "#265380", "Non-native Bees" = "#E3DE2D")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    coord_flip()
  
  print(p_family)
}

# Save all interaction plots (uncomment to save)
# ggsave("bee_flower_interactions.png", p_interactions, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("bee_flower_interactions_stacked.png", p_interactions_stacked, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("bee_flower_heatmap.png", p_heatmap, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)



# Export interaction data for further analysis
#write.csv(interaction_summary, "bee_flower_interaction_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#write.csv(bee_flower_interactions, "bee_flower_interactions_detailed.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nAnalysis complete! Results exported to:\n")
cat("- bee_flower_interaction_summary.csv\n")
cat("- bee_flower_interactions_detailed.csv\n")
cat("\nKey findings saved in the console output above.\n")
```

Proportion of native bees at site types
```{r}
# Native bees across site types analyses

# Add treatment type classification based on Site column
# Treatment (T): sites where second letter is T
# Negative Control (N): sites where second letter is N  
# Positive Control (P): sites where second letter is P

bee_site_analysis <- bees_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Site_Type = case_when(
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "T" ~ "Treatment",
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "N" ~ "Negative Control",
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "P" ~ "Positive Control",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    Site_Type = factor(Site_Type, levels = c("Treatment", "Negative Control", "Positive Control"))
  ) %>%
  filter(Site_Type != "Other")  # Remove any unclassified sites

# Check the distribution
cat("========== SITE TYPE DISTRIBUTION ==========\n")
cat("Total bee captures by site type:\n")
print(table(bee_site_analysis$Site_Type))
cat("\nBreakdown by site:\n")
print(table(bee_site_analysis$Site, bee_site_analysis$Site_Type))
cat("\n")

# Calculate proportions of native bees by site type
bee_site_proportions <- bee_site_analysis %>%
  group_by(Site_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_captures = n(),
    native_captures = sum(nativity == 1),
    non_native_captures = sum(nativity == 0),
    prop_native = native_captures / total_captures * 100,
    prop_non_native = non_native_captures / total_captures * 100,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

cat("========== NATIVE BEE PROPORTIONS BY SITE TYPE ==========\n")
print(bee_site_proportions)
cat("\n")

# Site type differences tests (ehh)

# Create contingency table for site type vs bee nativity
site_contingency <- table(bee_site_analysis$Site_Type, bee_site_analysis$nativity)
colnames(site_contingency) <- c("Non-native", "Native")

cat("========== CONTINGENCY TABLE ==========\n")
print(site_contingency)
cat("\n")

# Chi-square test for independence
site_chi_result <- chisq.test(site_contingency)
cat("========== CHI-SQUARE TEST RESULTS ==========\n")
cat("Chi-square statistic:", site_chi_result$statistic, "\n")
cat("Degrees of freedom:", site_chi_result$parameter, "\n")
cat("P-value:", site_chi_result$p.value, "\n")

# Calculate effect size (Cramer's V)
site_cramers_v <- sqrt(site_chi_result$statistic / 
                         (sum(site_contingency) * (min(dim(site_contingency)) - 1)))
cat("Cramer's V (effect size):", site_cramers_v, "\n")

# Interpretation
if(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.001) {
  cat("Interpretation: Highly significant difference in native bee proportions across site types (p < 0.001)\n")
} else if(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.01) {
  cat("Interpretation: Very significant difference across site types (p < 0.01)\n")
} else if(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("Interpretation: Significant difference across site types (p < 0.05)\n")
} else {
  cat("Interpretation: No significant difference across site types (p >= 0.05)\n")
}
cat("\n")

# Pairwise comparison of sites

cat("========== PAIRWISE PROPORTION TESTS ==========\n")

# Function to perform pairwise comparisons
pairwise_site_comparisons <- function(data, site_type_col, nativity_col) {
  site_levels <- levels(data[[site_type_col]])
  results <- data.frame()
  
  for(i in 1:(length(site_levels)-1)) {
    for(j in (i+1):length(site_levels)) {
      group1 <- data[data[[site_type_col]] == site_levels[i], ]
      group2 <- data[data[[site_type_col]] == site_levels[j], ]
      
      prop_test <- prop.test(
        x = c(sum(group1[[nativity_col]] == 1), sum(group2[[nativity_col]] == 1)),
        n = c(nrow(group1), nrow(group2))
      )
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        Comparison = paste(site_levels[i], "vs", site_levels[j]),
        Native_Prop_1 = sum(group1[[nativity_col]] == 1) / nrow(group1) * 100,
        Native_Prop_2 = sum(group2[[nativity_col]] == 1) / nrow(group2) * 100,
        Difference = abs(sum(group1[[nativity_col]] == 1) / nrow(group1) - 
                         sum(group2[[nativity_col]] == 1) / nrow(group2)) * 100,
        P_value = prop_test$p.value,
        CI_lower = prop_test$conf.int[1] * 100,
        CI_upper = prop_test$conf.int[2] * 100
      ))
    }
  }
  return(results)
}

pairwise_results <- pairwise_site_comparisons(bee_site_analysis, "Site_Type", "nativity")
print(pairwise_results)

# Adjust p-values for multiple comparisons (Bonferroni correction)
pairwise_results$P_adjusted <- p.adjust(pairwise_results$P_value, method = "bonferroni")
cat("\n========== PAIRWISE TESTS WITH BONFERRONI CORRECTION ==========\n")
print(pairwise_results)

# Bootstrap confidence interval for site categories

set.seed(123)
bootstrap_site_props <- function(data, site_type, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
  site_data <- data %>% filter(Site_Type == site_type)
  n_total <- nrow(site_data)
  
  native_props <- replicate(n_bootstrap, {
    boot_sample <- sample(site_data$nativity, n_total, replace = TRUE)
    sum(boot_sample == 1) / n_total * 100
  })
  
  return(quantile(native_props, c(0.025, 0.975)))
}

treatment_ci <- bootstrap_site_props(bee_site_analysis, "Treatment")
negative_ci <- bootstrap_site_props(bee_site_analysis, "Negative Control")
positive_ci <- bootstrap_site_props(bee_site_analysis, "Positive Control")

# Add confidence intervals to proportion data
bee_site_proportions <- bee_site_proportions %>%
  mutate(
    lower_ci = case_when(
      Site_Type == "Treatment" ~ treatment_ci[1],
      Site_Type == "Negative Control" ~ negative_ci[1],
      Site_Type == "Positive Control" ~ positive_ci[1]
    ),
    upper_ci = case_when(
      Site_Type == "Treatment" ~ treatment_ci[2],
      Site_Type == "Negative Control" ~ negative_ci[2],
      Site_Type == "Positive Control" ~ positive_ci[2]
    )
  )

# Plots

# Plot 1: Native bee proportions by site type with error bars
p_site <- ggplot(bee_site_proportions, aes(x = Site_Type, y = prop_native, fill = Site_Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci), width = 0.25, size = 0.8) +
  # Add significance annotation from overall test
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = max(bee_site_proportions$upper_ci) + 5,
           label = ifelse(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                         ifelse(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(site_chi_result$p.value < 0.05, "*", "n.s."))),
           size = 8, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Native Bee Proportions by Site Type",
    subtitle = paste0("Based on ", nrow(bee_site_analysis), " bee captures | Chi-square p-value: ", 
                      format(site_chi_result$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)),
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Proportion of Native Bees (%)",
    fill = "Site Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Treatment" = "#265380", 
                               "Negative Control" = "#E3DE2D", 
                               "Positive Control" = "#8B7355")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, min(100, max(bee_site_proportions$upper_ci) + 10)),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

print(p_site)

# Plot 2: Stacked bar chart showing composition
p_site_stacked <- ggplot(bee_site_proportions, aes(x = Site_Type, y = prop_native, fill = "Native")) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = prop_non_native, fill = "Non-native"), stat = "identity", width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(y = prop_native/2, label = paste0(round(prop_native, 1), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 4.5) +
  geom_text(aes(y = prop_native + prop_non_native/2, 
                label = paste0(round(prop_non_native, 1), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Bee Nativity Composition by Site Type",
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Proportion (%)",
    fill = "Bee Nativity"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native" = "#265380", "Non-native" = "#E3DE2D")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(p_site_stacked)

# Plot 3: Bees captured per site type (boxplot of counts)
bee_counts_by_site <- bee_site_analysis %>%
  group_by(Site, Site_Type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

p_counts <- ggplot(bee_counts_by_site, aes(x = Site_Type, y = count, fill = Site_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Bee Captures by Site Type",
    subtitle = "Number of bees captured per site",
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Number of Bee Captures",
    fill = "Site Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Treatment" = "#265380", 
                               "Negative Control" = "#E3DE2D", 
                               "Positive Control" = "#8B7355")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(p_counts)

# Native bee proportions over time by site type (scuffed)

# Add month information
bee_temporal <- bee_site_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    Month = format(Date, "%Y-%m"),
    Month_num = as.numeric(format(Date, "%m"))
  )

# Calculate monthly proportions by site type
monthly_proportions <- bee_temporal %>%
  group_by(Site_Type, Month, Month_num) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    native = sum(nativity == 1),
    prop_native = native / total * 100,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  filter(total >= 3)  # Only include months with at least 3 captures

if(nrow(monthly_proportions) > 0) {
  p_temporal <- ggplot(monthly_proportions, aes(x = Month, y = prop_native, 
                                                  color = Site_Type, group = Site_Type)) +
    geom_line(size = 1.2) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = "Native Bee Proportions Over Time by Site Type",
      x = "Month",
      y = "Proportion of Native Bees (%)",
      color = "Site Type"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Treatment" = "#265380", 
                                  "Negative Control" = "#E3DE2D", 
                                  "Positive Control" = "#8B7355")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    ylim(0, 100)
  
  print(p_temporal)
}


# Save results to CSV
#write.csv(bee_site_proportions, "bee_site_type_proportions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#write.csv(pairwise_results, "bee_site_type_pairwise_tests.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save plots (uncomment to save)
# ggsave("bee_site_type_comparison.png", p_site, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("bee_site_type_stacked.png", p_site_stacked, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("bee_capture_counts.png", p_counts, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

cat("\n========== SUMMARY OF SITE TYPE ANALYSIS ==========\n")
cat("Analysis complete! Results exported to:\n")
cat("- bee_site_type_proportions.csv\n")
cat("- bee_site_type_pairwise_tests.csv\n")
cat("\nKey findings:\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(bee_site_proportions)) {
  cat(sprintf("%s: %.1f%% native bees (n=%d)\n", 
              bee_site_proportions$Site_Type[i],
              bee_site_proportions$prop_native[i],
              bee_site_proportions$total_captures[i]))
}


```


```{r}
# Native plants across site type

# Add treatment type classification based on Site column for flowers
flower_site_analysis <- flowers_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Site_Type = case_when(
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "T" ~ "Treatment",
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "N" ~ "Negative Control",
      substr(Site, 2, 2) == "P" ~ "Positive Control",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    Site_Type = factor(Site_Type, levels = c("Treatment", "Negative Control", "Positive Control"))
  ) %>%
  filter(Site_Type != "Other")  # Remove any unclassified sites

# Check the distribution
cat("========== FLOWER SITE TYPE DISTRIBUTION ==========\n")
cat("Total flower observations by site type:\n")
print(table(flower_site_analysis$Site_Type))
cat("\nBreakdown by site:\n")
print(table(flower_site_analysis$Site, flower_site_analysis$Site_Type))
cat("\n")

# Calculate proportions of native plants by site type
flower_site_proportions <- flower_site_analysis %>%
  group_by(Site_Type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_observations = n(),
    native_observations = sum(nativity == 1),
    non_native_observations = sum(nativity == 0),
    prop_native = native_observations / total_observations * 100,
    prop_non_native = non_native_observations / total_observations * 100,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

cat("========== NATIVE PLANT PROPORTIONS BY SITE TYPE ==========\n")
print(flower_site_proportions)
cat("\n")

# Site type differences

# Create contingency table for site type vs plant nativity
flower_site_contingency <- table(flower_site_analysis$Site_Type, flower_site_analysis$nativity)
colnames(flower_site_contingency) <- c("Non-native", "Native")

cat("========== CONTINGENCY TABLE (PLANTS) ==========\n")
print(flower_site_contingency)
cat("\n")

# Chi-square test for independence
flower_site_chi <- chisq.test(flower_site_contingency)
cat("========== CHI-SQUARE TEST RESULTS (PLANTS) ==========\n")
cat("Chi-square statistic:", flower_site_chi$statistic, "\n")
cat("Degrees of freedom:", flower_site_chi$parameter, "\n")
cat("P-value:", flower_site_chi$p.value, "\n")

# Calculate effect size (Cramer's V)
flower_site_cramers_v <- sqrt(flower_site_chi$statistic / 
                                (sum(flower_site_contingency) * 
                                   (min(dim(flower_site_contingency)) - 1)))
cat("Cramer's V (effect size):", flower_site_cramers_v, "\n")

# Interpretation
if(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.001) {
  cat("Interpretation: Highly significant difference in native plant proportions across site types (p < 0.001)\n")
} else if(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.01) {
  cat("Interpretation: Very significant difference across site types (p < 0.01)\n")
} else if(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("Interpretation: Significant difference across site types (p < 0.05)\n")
} else {
  cat("Interpretation: No significant difference across site types (p >= 0.05)\n")
}
cat("\n")

# Pairwise comp for plants

cat("========== PAIRWISE PROPORTION TESTS (PLANTS) ==========\n")

flower_pairwise_results <- pairwise_site_comparisons(flower_site_analysis, "Site_Type", "nativity")
print(flower_pairwise_results)

# Adjust p-values for multiple comparisons
flower_pairwise_results$P_adjusted <- p.adjust(flower_pairwise_results$P_value, method = "bonferroni")
cat("\n========== PAIRWISE TESTS WITH BONFERRONI CORRECTION (PLANTS) ==========\n")
print(flower_pairwise_results)

# Bootstrap for plants

set.seed(123)
bootstrap_flower_site_props <- function(data, site_type, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
  site_data <- data %>% filter(Site_Type == site_type)
  n_total <- nrow(site_data)
  
  native_props <- replicate(n_bootstrap, {
    boot_sample <- sample(site_data$nativity, n_total, replace = TRUE)
    sum(boot_sample == 1) / n_total * 100
  })
  
  return(quantile(native_props, c(0.025, 0.975)))
}

flower_treatment_ci <- bootstrap_flower_site_props(flower_site_analysis, "Treatment")
flower_negative_ci <- bootstrap_flower_site_props(flower_site_analysis, "Negative Control")
flower_positive_ci <- bootstrap_flower_site_props(flower_site_analysis, "Positive Control")

# Add confidence intervals to proportion data
flower_site_proportions <- flower_site_proportions %>%
  mutate(
    lower_ci = case_when(
      Site_Type == "Treatment" ~ flower_treatment_ci[1],
      Site_Type == "Negative Control" ~ flower_negative_ci[1],
      Site_Type == "Positive Control" ~ flower_positive_ci[1]
    ),
    upper_ci = case_when(
      Site_Type == "Treatment" ~ flower_treatment_ci[2],
      Site_Type == "Negative Control" ~ flower_negative_ci[2],
      Site_Type == "Positive Control" ~ flower_positive_ci[2]
    )
  )

# Plots

# Plot 1: Native plant proportions by site type with error bars
p_flower_site <- ggplot(flower_site_proportions, aes(x = Site_Type, y = prop_native, fill = Site_Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower_ci, ymax = upper_ci), width = 0.25, size = 0.8) +
  # Add significance annotation from overall test
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = max(flower_site_proportions$upper_ci) + 5,
           label = ifelse(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.001, "***",
                         ifelse(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.01, "**",
                               ifelse(flower_site_chi$p.value < 0.05, "*", "n.s."))),
           size = 8, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Native Plant Proportions by Site Type",
    subtitle = paste0("Based on ", nrow(flower_site_analysis), " flower observations | Chi-square p-value: ", 
                      format(flower_site_chi$p.value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)),
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Proportion of Native Plants (%)",
    fill = "Site Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Treatment" = "#2E8B57", 
                               "Negative Control" = "#DAA520", 
                               "Positive Control" = "#8B4513")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, min(100, max(flower_site_proportions$upper_ci) + 10)),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

print(p_flower_site)

# Plot 2: Stacked bar chart for plants
p_flower_stacked <- ggplot(flower_site_proportions, aes(x = Site_Type, y = prop_native, fill = "Native")) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = prop_non_native, fill = "Non-native"), stat = "identity", width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(y = prop_native/2, label = paste0(round(prop_native, 1), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 4.5) +
  geom_text(aes(y = prop_native + prop_non_native/2, 
                label = paste0(round(prop_non_native, 1), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Plant Nativity Composition by Site Type",
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Proportion (%)",
    fill = "Plant Nativity"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Native" = "#2E8B57", "Non-native" = "#DAA520")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(p_flower_stacked)

# Plot 3: Flower observations per site type (boxplot)
flower_counts_by_site <- flower_site_analysis %>%
  group_by(Site, Site_Type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = 'drop')

p_flower_counts <- ggplot(flower_counts_by_site, aes(x = Site_Type, y = count, fill = Site_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, width = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Flower Observations by Site Type",
    subtitle = "Number of flowering units observed per site",
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Number of Flower Observations",
    fill = "Site Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Treatment" = "#2E8B57", 
                               "Negative Control" = "#DAA520", 
                               "Positive Control" = "#8B4513")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(p_flower_counts)

# Combined analysis: Plants AND Bees by Site Type

# Combine plant and bee data for comparison
combined_site_data <- bind_rows(
  flower_site_analysis %>%
    select(Site_Type, nativity) %>%
    mutate(Organism = "Plants"),
  bee_site_analysis %>%
    select(Site_Type, nativity) %>%
    mutate(Organism = "Bees")
)

# Calculate proportions for combined visualization
combined_site_proportions <- combined_site_data %>%
  group_by(Site_Type, Organism) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    native = sum(nativity == 1),
    prop_native = native / total * 100,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Plot 4: Comparison of plants vs bees across site types
p_combined_site <- ggplot(combined_site_proportions, 
                          aes(x = Site_Type, y = prop_native, fill = Organism)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
           width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Native Proportions: Plants vs Bees by Site Type",
    subtitle = "Comparing native proportions across different experimental treatments",
    x = "Site Type",
    y = "Proportion of Native Species (%)",
    fill = "Organism Type"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Plants" = "#2E8B57", "Bees" = "#265380")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, color = "grey50", size = 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10))

print(p_combined_site)



#write.csv(flower_site_proportions, "plant_site_type_proportions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#write.csv(flower_pairwise_results, "plant_site_type_pairwise_tests.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n========== SUMMARY OF PLANT SITE TYPE ANALYSIS ==========\n")
cat("Analysis complete! Results exported to:\n")
cat("- plant_site_type_proportions.csv\n")
cat("- plant_site_type_pairwise_tests.csv\n")
cat("\nKey findings for plants:\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(flower_site_proportions)) {
  cat(sprintf("%s: %.1f%% native plants (n=%d)\n", 
              flower_site_proportions$Site_Type[i],
              flower_site_proportions$prop_native[i],
              flower_site_proportions$total_observations[i]))
}

# Plant vs bee nativity proportions across site types

cat("\n========== COMPARISON OF PLANTS AND BEES BY SITE TYPE ==========\n")
comparison_by_site <- combined_site_proportions %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Organism, values_from = c(prop_native, total)) %>%
  mutate(
    Difference = prop_native_Bees - prop_native_Plants,
    Direction = ifelse(Difference > 0, "Bees higher", 
                      ifelse(Difference < 0, "Plants higher", "Equal"))
  )

print(comparison_by_site)

# Test if plants and bees differ within each site type
cat("\n========== STATISTICAL TESTS: PLANTS VS BEES WITHIN EACH SITE TYPE ==========\n")
for(site in c("Treatment", "Negative Control", "Positive Control")) {
  plant_data <- flower_site_analysis %>% filter(Site_Type == site)
  bee_data <- bee_site_analysis %>% filter(Site_Type == site)
  
  if(nrow(plant_data) > 0 & nrow(bee_data) > 0) {
    contingency <- matrix(c(
      sum(plant_data$nativity == 1), sum(plant_data$nativity == 0),
      sum(bee_data$nativity == 1), sum(bee_data$nativity == 0)
    ), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
    
    rownames(contingency) <- c("Plants", "Bees")
    colnames(contingency) <- c("Native", "Non-native")
    
    test_result <- chisq.test(contingency)
    cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n", site))
    cat("  Plants: ", sprintf("%.1f%% native", sum(plant_data$nativity == 1)/nrow(plant_data)*100), "\n")
    cat("  Bees: ", sprintf("%.1f%% native", sum(bee_data$nativity == 1)/nrow(bee_data)*100), "\n")
    cat("  P-value:", test_result$p.value, "\n")
    cat("  Significant:", ifelse(test_result$p.value < 0.05, "YES", "NO"), "\n")
  }
}
```
